idea.hook: The Universe has TWO expansion rates?!
facts: Local measurements (Type Ia supernovae) calculate the universe's expansion at ~73 km/s/Mpc.
Early universe data (Cosmic Microwave Background) predicts a rate closer to ~67 km/s/Mpc.
This ~9% difference, known as the 'Hubble Tension,' keeps growing more statistically significant!
idea.payoff: A fundamental conflict in cosmology challenging our most accepted models of the universe's origins and fate!
idea.title: Universe's Expansion Paradox! 🤔🌌

scenes.text: The Universe has TWO expansion rates?!
scenes.visual: Swirling galaxy cluster background, slightly pulsing. Text appears boldly.
Image of a Type Ia supernova explosion in a distant galaxy. A number '73' glows.
A map of the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) with warm/cool spots. A number '67' glows.
The numbers '73' and '67' appear, then a large red '9%' difference sign appears between them, with a '?' overlaying a cosmic background.

scenes.text: Challenging everything we know about the Universe!
scenes.visual: A vast, unknowable cosmic expanse. Distant galaxies fade into an encompassing darkness, emphasizing the mystery. Text pulses with a subtle glow.
